signals.title: AUD/USD — NAB Hike Call + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7247 following NAB's call for a June RBA hike to 4.60% amid Middle East-driven inflation. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 87% short — institutional long conviction is being faded by extreme retail shorting. This divergence at the 0.7250 level suggests a squeeze higher if retail capitulates. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry advantage to AUD, further supporting the bid.
signals.watch level: Watch for a sustained break above 0.7250 to trigger retail stop-outs.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.31 as JPY gains ground following Trump's pause on Project Freedom and easing Hormuz tensions. Despite the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%), JPY COT LF net short remains crowded at -88,543. The pair is retreating from recent highs as intervention caution persists. Carry remains the structural driver, but geopolitical de-escalation and BoJ hike bias (OIS) limit USD upside.
signals.watch level: Next BoJ meeting on 16 June for confirmation of the hike bias.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Labor Data
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5946 as Q1 labor data stabilization gives RBNZ room to maintain its hawkish stance. COT LF net short -16,833 (specs short) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias (OIS). This positioning-policy divergence creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ signals a hike on 27 May. NZD is currently the G8 leader (1W avg +0.498%), outperforming despite a -150bp carry deficit vs USD.
signals.watch level: RBNZ meeting on 27 May is the primary catalyst for a spec unwind.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Yield Spread
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1737 showing a significant correlation break vs EuroStoxx (corr=+0.717 vs norm=-0.149, z=+1.99σ). The pair is decoupling from equity flows, driven instead by the narrowing US-DE 10Y yield spread (US 4.42% vs DE 3.11%). While the 175bp Fed-ECB carry spread favors USD, the pause in Project Freedom has removed immediate USD safe-haven premiums, allowing EUR to test the 1.1750 handle.
signals.watch level: Watch for ECB meeting on 11 June to redefine the rate path.
